academic_profile: 3.9 unweighted GPA, 1450 PSAT, taking AP Calc BC, AP Physics 1, AP Lang this year. Expecting 1500+ SAT.
academic_strengths: Math and physics. Strongest in anything quantitative. Teachers say he picks up new concepts fast.
academic_weaknesses: Writing is functional but not a strength. Procrastinates on long-term projects.
extracurriculars: Robotics team (VEX, 3 years, wants captain senior year), varsity tennis, math honor society, part-time job at a bagel shop on weekends
interests: Robotics, 3D printing, builds mechanical keyboards and sells them online, Formula 1
personality: Driven but laid back about it. Competitive with himself more than others. Gets deep into whatever he's interested in.
teacher_quote: His physics teacher said he asks the questions she wishes the whole class would ask.
relocation: Yes, anywhere for the right school
geographic_preference: No strong preference, slight lean Southeast
must_haves: Strong engineering program, real research opportunities for undergrads
schools_on_radar: Stanford, MIT, Georgia Tech, Case Western, University of Florida
priority_other: We make too much for need-based aid but $85K a year is not happening. Merit money matters.
additional_context: Brett wants mechanical or aerospace engineering. We've heard Florida Bright Futures covers a lot if he stays in state, want to understand that vs. chasing merit out of state.
